{'summary': 'Exploitation of .NET AppDomain hijacking and scheduled task manipulation for malware persistence, combined with AI-assisted malware development and SEO poisoning delivery vectors.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['.NET Runtime', 'Zoom Client', 'Oracle SQL Developer', 'OnlyOffice'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Disable macro execution in downloaded archives', 'Enforce strict software procurement policies', 'Monitor outbound traffic for known C2 domains', 'Implement DNS filtering to block malicious SEO-poisoned domains'], 'references': ['Check Point Research Report on Nimbus Manticore', 'SecurityAffairs Article: Nimbus Manticore Expanded Attacks With AI-Assisted Malware and Fake Zoom Installers'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'The threat actor leverages .NET AppDomain hijacking to silently load rogue DLLs under trusted processes, and monitors/hijacks legitimate Zoom scheduled tasks to establish persistence. {'summary': 'AI Governance Gap: Treating AI compliance as a post-development review layer rather than integrated release infrastructure, leading to outdated controls, unmonitored agent actions, and regulatory risk.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Generative AI systems', 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines', 'AI Agents', 'CI/CD deployment pipelines'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Implement manual pre-deployment compliance checks', 'Use third-party AI risk assessment tools', 'Restrict AI agent permissions to read-only modes'], 'references': ['NIST AI Risk Management Framework', 'EU AI Act', 'China Generative AI Regulations', 'CSO Online Article: Stop treating AI governance as a review layer. {'summary': 'Widespread exploitation of unpatched software vulnerabilities, particularly in perimeter and edge devices, has become the primary initial access vector for cyber breaches.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Perimeter devices', 'Edge devices', 'Third-party software', 'Open-source components'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Network microsegmentation', 'Continuous real-time threat monitoring', 'Strict access controls and identity management'], 'references': ['Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) 2025/2026', 'Google Cloud Security Cloud Threat Horizons Report', 'Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) Zero-Day Report'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'The article highlights a systemic trend where exploited flaws account for 31% of breaches, surpassing credential abuse. Only 26% of critical vulnerabilities and CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEVs) are fully remediated, with median patch times rising to 43 days. The attack chain often begins with vulnerability exploitation for initial access, followed by credential theft for lateral movement and data exfiltration.', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Implement risk-based, continuous vulnerability management tied to real-time exploitation intelligence. {'summary': 'OAuth device code flow abuse and phishing campaigns enabling MFA bypass via Microsoft 365 token theft', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Microsoft 365', 'Microsoft OAuth Authentication Service'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Exclude emergency access accounts from device code flow restrictions to prevent lockouts', 'Train employees to recognize device code requests as red flags', 'Block authentication transfer policies'], 'references': ['FBI Warning on Kali365 Phishing-as-a-Service', 'Microsoft Advisory on Adversary-in-the-Middle Phishing', 'Arctic Wolf Detection Report', 'Gurucul Research on Kali365'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'Threat actors leverage phishing-as-a-service platforms (e.g., Kali365, EvilTokens) to conduct large-scale device code phishing campaigns. This grants attackers valid OAuth access and refresh tokens, completely bypassing multifactor authentication (MFA).
